title: Incorrect Memoization in Min Cost Climbing Stairs

statement.markdown: You are given an integer array cost where cost[i] is the cost of the i-th step. Each step i can be done once. You are at step 0, initially.
statement.inputFormat: The input to the function will be an integer array cost with size n, where 1 <= n <= 2000. Each element of cost is in the range [0, 999].
statement.outputFormat: Return the minimum cost to climb n steps. The answer is guaranteed to fit within a 32-bit integer.

examples.explanation: Because cost[0]=3 and cost[1]=2, the minimum cost to climb 2 steps is 1.

hints.text: Make sure to initialize the memoization dp array correctly.

editorial.title: Incorrect Memoization in Min Cost Climbing Stairs
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using memoization to store the minimum cost to reach each step.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the optimal solution can be proven by showing that the minimum cost to reach each step is the minimum cost to reach the previous step plus the cost of the current step.
commonMistakes: Not considering the minimum cost to reach each step.
